title: Antiderivative
short: A function whose derivative is the given one; the result of indefinite integration.
full:
An antiderivative of f is any function F whose derivative is f, that is F′ = f. Finding one is exactly what indefinite integration does, so 'antiderivative' and 'indefinite integral' name the same thing.

Antiderivatives are not unique: if F works, so does F + C for any constant C, because the derivative of a constant is zero. That family is why integrals carry '+ C'.

You can always check an integral by differentiating the answer — it should return the original integrand.
